Source code for killMS.Other.ModParsetType

from __future__ import absolute_import
from __future__ import division
from __future__ import print_function


[docs]
def ParsetToDict(fname:str) -> dict:
    '''
    Function to convert parset to dictionary.
    Returns Dict
    
    :param fname: Filename to read from
    :type fname: str
    '''
    f=file(fname,"r")
    Dict={}
    ListOut=f.readlines()
    order=[]
    i=0
    for line in ListOut:
        line=line.replace("\n","")
        if not("=" in line): continue
        if "#" in line: continue
        key,val=line.split("=")
        key=key.replace(" ","")
        key=key.replace(".","_")
        #Dict[key]={"id":i,"show":1, "col":"", "help": "", "val":val}
        Dict[key]={"id":i,"val":val}
        i+=1
    return Dict





[docs]
def DictToParset(Dict,
                 fout:str) -> None:
     '''
     Function to write Dict to Parset.
     
     :param Dict: Dictionary to write out
     :param fout: Filename to write Dict to
     :type fout: str
     '''
     f=open(fout,"w")
     ll=sorted(Dict.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]['id'])
     Lkeys=[ll[i][0] for i in range(len(ll))]
            
     for key in Lkeys:
         keyw=key.replace("_",".")
         f.write("%s = %s\n"%(keyw,Dict[key]["val"]))
     f.close()
